title: Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues after its engine burn to head to the moon. The mission remains on track as it cruises toward its lunar destination.

title: Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand
summary: Argotec opened its first U.S. satellite production facility near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to meet rising demand from American space programs. The facility will help the Italian company expand its presence in the growing U.S. space industry.

title: White House again proposes steep NASA budget cuts
summary: The White House has proposed significant budget cuts for NASA for the second year in a row, which would heavily impact the agency's science programs and the International Space Station.

title: SpaceX and Amazon spar over satellite deployments
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are in a dispute over Amazon's satellite deployment plans for its broadband constellation, with SpaceX raising space safety concerns. Amazon denies the claims and states it will revise its deployment strategies.

title: Europe’s strategic autonomy in space will define its role in the ‘second space age’
summary: Europe's strategic autonomy in space is crucial for its role in the 'second space age' as it seeks to reduce reliance on foreign technology. The European Union emphasizes the need for independent access, control, and development in space.

title: China’s commercial Tianlong-3 rocket fails on debut launch
summary: China's commercial Tianlong-3 rocket failed during its debut launch due to an anomaly during ascent. The failure marks a setback for Space Pioneer's ambitions in the commercial space sector.

title: Artemis 2 heads to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has launched toward the moon following a successful maneuver on April 2. This marks a significant step in the agency's lunar exploration program.

title: Swift spacecraft reorientation buys time for reboost mission
summary: NASA adjusted the operations of the Swift spacecraft to extend its time in a decaying orbit, allowing for a potential reboost mission later this year. This maneuver aims to prolong the spacecraft's operational lifespan.

title: Former Sierra Space CEO Tom Vice to lead Astrion
summary: Tom Vice, former CEO of Sierra Space, has been appointed to lead Astrion, a Huntsville-based defense contractor specializing in systems engineering and space mission assurance.

title: Optical terminals still a bottleneck in Pentagon’s proliferated constellation
summary: The Pentagon faces challenges with optical communication terminals for its proliferated satellite constellation, as current capabilities are insufficient. GP Sandhoo highlights the need for more optical terminals to meet requirements.

title: Disclosure day: If ET made contact, how would we handle the news?
summary: Experts discuss potential public and scientific reactions to first contact with an extraterrestrial civilization. Space.com explores hypothetical scenarios and expert opinions on how humanity might handle such an event.

title: Defender at 45: How an icon of space gaming is in danger of being lost to history
summary: The iconic arcade game 'Defender' turns 45 in 2026, raising concerns about its preservation for future generations. The article highlights the need to safeguard this classic space gaming title before it reaches its 50th anniversary.

title: An Artemis 2 astronaut took a 'bath' on camera on the way to the moon. Mission Control's reaction was priceless (video)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 pilot Victor Glover was caught on camera taking a 'bath' during a workout, leading to a humorous reaction from Mission Control.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts are now headed to the moon. Why has it taken humanity so long to go back?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission marks humanity's first crewed moon journey since 1972, raising questions about the 50-year gap in lunar exploration. The delay highlights shifts in space priorities, funding challenges, and technological advancements since the Apollo era.

title: It's official: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will break humanity's all-time distance record
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will break the all-time distance record from Earth, reaching 252,757 miles (406,773 kilometers) on April 6. This surpasses the previous record set by Apollo 13 in April 1970.

title: Astronauts on the ISS watched NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch from space
summary: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station (ISS) observed NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch from space. The event marked a significant milestone as it was witnessed from both Earth and low Earth orbit.

title: Venus shines at its best in spring and summer 2026 — here's what to look for
summary: Venus will be prominently visible as an evening star in 2026, offering dazzling conjunctions and peak brightness throughout spring and summer.

title: 1st results from Blue Ghost lunar lander reveal how much we still don't know about the moon
summary: Initial data from Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost lunar lander challenges the long-held belief that the moon's near side is hotter than its far side. The findings suggest a more complex thermal distribution than previously understood.

title: Watch Atlas V rocket launch its heaviest-ever payload early on April 4
summary: An Atlas V rocket is set to launch 29 of Amazon's internet satellites on April 4, marking the heaviest payload the rocket has ever carried. The launch can be watched live.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts see Earth in the rear-view mirror | Space photo of the day for April 3, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a stunning photo of Earth as they traveled away from it toward the Moon. This marks a significant milestone in their lunar mission preparations.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch
summary: NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting April 8 for the CRS-24 resupply launch to the ISS. The Cygnus XL spacecraft, carrying 11,000 pounds of cargo, will launch aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers
summary: NASA has appointed its first Artemis II science officers—Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia—to integrate advanced lunar science into Mission Control, shaping future crew observations and analysis of the Moon.

title: Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom
summary: The news commemorates the 100th anniversary of Virgil I. 'Gus' Grissom's birth, highlighting his role as one of NASA's first seven astronauts and America's second astronaut to fly in space aboard Liberty Bell 7 in 1961.

title: Hello, World
summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, commander of the Artemis II mission, captured a stunning image of Earth from the Orion spacecraft, showcasing two auroras and zodiacal light. This is the first photo of Earth taken during the translunar injection burn of the Artemis II mission.

title: Barents Sea Tied to Low Arctic Sea Ice
summary: Patches of open water in the Barents Sea contributed to the lowest Arctic sea ice extent in March 2026, tying with the lowest maximum observed in satellite records.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Leaves Earth Orbit for Flight around Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully completed a translunar injection burn, propelling the Orion spacecraft and its crew toward the Moon. This marks the first time in over 50 years that astronauts will fly around the Moon.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission will launch four astronauts on a 10-day journey around the Moon and back aboard the Orion spacecraft. The mission, scheduled for April 1, 2026, includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Liftoff! NASA Launches Astronauts on Historic Artemis Moon Mission
summary: NASA successfully launched the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The SLS rocket lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, carrying four astronauts on a historic journey around the Moon.

title: March of the Harmattan
summary: Strong winds in March 2026 carried Saharan dust across northwestern Africa and toward the Canary Islands, reducing visibility and prompting alerts.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads to the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets from Florida, California, and Russia. This marks a busy period for the space industry with multiple high-profile missions.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights advancements in space technology and lunar mission preparations.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit as part of the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, marking a significant shift in its lunar exploration strategy. The agency also indicated a pivot away from the Lunar Gateway project.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Ottobrunn, Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company had planned to proceed with the launch but faced an issue that led to the cancellation.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed the plans for this significant initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to Starbase launch pads in preparation for the next generation of Starship rockets, known as Block 3. This effort highlights SpaceX's focus on increasing launch frequency and operational readiness.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets carrying internet satellites. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed with versatile capabilities for space applications. The innovation is expected to enhance performance and reduce costs in satellite and spacecraft systems.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly caused a loss of communication. The mission team confirmed the successful recovery of the spacecraft.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), informing industry about planned activities and opportunities across ESA programmes.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to hunt for Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in Europe's largest cryovacuum chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers in financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky traditional antennas.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, maintaining the same funding level as the 2026 request while reducing allocations for science and STEM missions.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper'. The issue was highlighted during an industry seminar.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and charts defining the space industry in Q1 2026, highlighting a resurgence in Earth Observation (EO) activities. It provides insights into the sector's growth and technological advancements.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed lunar landings starting next year, exciting CLPS companies eager to participate. The initiative, dubbed an 'Opportunity Bomb,' aims to accelerate lunar exploration and infrastructure development.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with four astronauts, including the first woman and person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The crew includes Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding. The company plans to build an uncrewed space station to host manufacturing infrastructure in orbit.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has raised $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with participation from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed support for establishing a Moon base as a strategic step towards future Mars missions. The comments highlight growing enthusiasm for the Artemis program and lunar exploration.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager Space and Icarus Robotics are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), marking a significant step in space robotics development. This partnership highlights the growing role of private companies in advancing ISS capabilities.

title: Live in the Booth: Matt Desch Talks Iridium NTN Direct and Alt PNT Advances
summary: Iridium is launching Iridium NTN Direct, a standardized and global narrow-band satellite IoT service, led by CEO Matt Desch. This initiative positions Iridium as the first in this space.

title: Redwire Wins European Quantum Satellite Contract
summary: Redwire has won a contract to develop a quantum-secure satellite for the European Space Agency's Quantum Key Distribution Satellite (QKDSat) program.

title: African Market for Satellite Services Offers Pent Up Demand
summary: Africa's large landmass of 11.7 million square miles presents challenges for satellite services, but demand is growing as of September 2025.

title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Globalstar, According to FT Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly in discussions to acquire Globalstar, according to a Financial Times report. The potential deal was first reported by FT on Wednesday evening.

title: Gravitics Receives Strategic Funding Increase From SpaceWERX
summary: Gravitics secured a Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract from SpaceWERX to accelerate the development of its Orbital Carrier architecture. This funding aims to support the U.S. Space Force's innovation initiatives.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate on a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing to safely remove satellites from orbit.

title: In-Orbit Logistics Companies Call for Clearer Demand Signals from Gov
summary: In-orbit logistics companies are urging the government to provide clearer demand signals. The Space Development Agency highlights the challenges of operating in space.

title: Canada’s Northwestel to Provide Broadband With Telesat Lightspeed Capacity
summary: Canada's Northwestel has signed a multi-year contract with Telesat to use capacity from its Lightspeed constellation for broadband services. Northwestel serves remote regions including Nunavut and the Northwest Territories.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Component Supplier Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space Corporation acquired Thermal Management Technologies (TMT) to support its orbital data center constellation development and vertical integration strategy.

title: ArkEdge Space to Work With ABIT For IoT Service Rollout
summary: ArkEdge Space and ABIT Corporation signed a memorandum of understanding to jointly develop IoT solutions and satellite communication devices. The partnership aims to advance satellite-based IoT services.
